what_it_does: It is a generated surface specification for UI view A5: it records the view's identity, title, platforms, represented concerns, journey membership, UI addresses, and build targets (components, lint command, token source).
observations: The view is titled 'Run in progress - ran/cached/stuck' and is in status 'planned'.
It is anchored to home journey J1 and also lists J7, with J7 marked as a cross_journey.
It declares 19 ui:// addresses spanning regions such as canvas, chat, inspector, toolbar, and run.
The build_to block names components, a design-lint command, and a token source path.
It represents four abstract tags: RUN-run, RUN-status, ENG-memo, and ENG-stuck.
standouts: Large cross-cutting address list touches many UI regions for a single view.
Explicit cross_journeys field separates shared journey wiring from the home journey.
build_to mixes implementation concerns: components, lint gate, and token source.
The directory path encodes the home journey (J1) as a spine location.
conventions: JSON manifest with id, area, title, status, platforms, and journey lists.
Directory naming mirrors home_journey for the blueprint spine.
Addresses use a ui:// URI scheme grouped by UI region.
journeys and cross_journeys are kept separate.
A top-level _what comment explains provenance and constraints.
concerns: Status is 'planned', so the spec may not yet reflect implemented reality.
The build_to lint command targets canvas/app/src, which may not match all declared regions.
Token source is written as a transformation arrow rather than a concrete build field.
The meaning of the 'represents' tags is not defined inside the file.
notes: A5 is a planned desktop/mobile surface spec for the run-status view, anchored to J1 and wired to J7, enumerating UI addresses and build targets.
questions: What exactly do RUN-run, RUN-status, ENG-memo, and ENG-stuck represent?
How are the 19 ui:// addresses validated against actual components?
What is B3, referenced as the web that journeys/cross_journeys carry?
Is the design_lint command expected to be run automatically or manually?
What does 'the FORM gate' refer to?
How do desktop and mobile differ when the same address list is used?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a generated surface spec that lists the view's journeys, addresses, and build targets for A5.
apparent_themes: Explicit separation of home and cross journeys
Build-time coupling of lint and tokens to surface specs
intention_signals: It tries to create a single canonical blueprint for a view so that journeys, UI addresses, components, lint, and tokens can be coordinated consistently across platforms.
novelty: The journey-spine directory layout, explicit cross_journey list, and broad ui:// address map across many UI regions for one view.
contribution: It contributes a structured per-view manifest that names the view, its journeys, the UI addresses it touches, and its build artifacts, which a fused system could keep as a surface-level contract.
summary_for_embedding: company/design/blueprint/surfaces/J1/A5.json is a planned generated surface specification for the A5 'Run in progress - ran/cached/stuck' desktop/mobile view, anchored to journey J1 with cross-journey J7, listing ui:// addresses and build targets.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to provide a canonical, buildable blueprint of the UI addresses, journeys, components, lint step, and token source for the A5 run-status surface.
